# Ivan Magda

> Personal blog about AI Engineering and Software Development by Ivan Magda.

## About

- [About](https://ivanmagda.dev/about/): Background, projects, and contact info

## Blog Series: Building a Coding Agent in Swift

A 9-part series documenting a Swift reimplementation of a Claude Code-style CLI agent.

- [Part 0: Bootstrapping the Project](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s00-bootstrapping-the-project/): Setting up a two-target SPM package, strict concurrency, and AsyncHTTPClient
- [Part 1: The Agent Loop](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s01-the-agent-loop/): The agent loop that drives everything — how a single stopReason check turns an API client into an autonomous agent
- [Part 2: Tool Dispatch](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s02-tool-dispatch/): Dictionary-based tool dispatch, path sandboxing, and file tools
- [Part 3: Self-Managed Task Tracking](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s03-self-managed-task-tracking/): A Codable todo system with validation constraints and nag reminders
- [Part 4: Subagents](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s04-subagents/): Context isolation through recursive agent loops with restricted tool sets
- [Part 5: Skill Loading](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s05-skill-loading/): Two-layer knowledge injection — system prompt awareness and on-demand tool results
- [Part 6: Context Compaction](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s06-context-compaction/): Three-layer compression strategy for infinite conversations
- [Part 7: Task System](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s07-task-system/): A file-based task DAG with dependency resolution
- [Part 8: Background Tasks](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/s08-background-tasks/): Swift actors for background execution with notification injection

## Synthesis: Agent Architecture Patterns

Cross-cutting essays that connect multiple parts of the Swift coding-agent series into single design arguments.

- [Reliability Patterns in the Agent Loop](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/loop-reliability/): How the agent loop, tool dispatch, and task tracking share one reliability problem that only appears when you hold them together
- [The Context Window Is a Budget](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/context-budget-skill-compaction/): Treating the context window as a finite budget changes how you design skill loading and compaction together
- [Durable Multi-Agent Work](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/durable-multi-agent-orchestration/): How subagent isolation, a file-persisted task DAG, and background execution compose into orchestration that stays coherent across time

## Blog Series: Reading Koog

A series reflecting on JetBrains' Koog framework after hand-rolling a Claude Code clone in Swift.

- [Part 1: What I Missed Until I Read Koog](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/koog-s01-foundation/): After nine posts building a Claude Code-style agent by hand in Swift, the foundation a framework like Koog gives you for free

## Synthesis: Apple Platforms after WWDC 2026

Cross-cutting essays that connect announcements from the WWDC 2026 Platforms State of the Union into single design arguments.

- [Three Ways to Run a Model on Apple Platforms](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/apple-platform-ai-model-strategy/): Foundation Models, Private Cloud Compute, and Core AI are a privacy/control/cost decision, not a capability ladder
- [The Agent Abstraction, Top to Bottom](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/dynamic-profiles-xcode-agents/): Dynamic Profiles in your app and plugins in Xcode are the same agent abstraction applied on both sides of the IDE
- [End-to-End Swift AI](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/end-to-end-swift-ai-device-to-server/): Open-sourcing the Foundation Models framework collapses the app/backend AI split into one Swift API from device to server
- [Designing for a Range, Not a Device](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/designing-for-a-range-adaptive-apps/): Adaptivity became the default — resizability, Liquid Glass, priority toolbars — plus a coding-agent skill to retrofit the rest
- [Becoming Part of the System](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/app-intents-on-screen-awareness/): App Intents schemas and the View Annotations API are two contracts — discoverability and actionability — you need both

## Standalone Posts

- [Fixing the 40k CLAUDE.md Warning in a Monorepo](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/fixing-40k-claude-md-warning-monorepo/): How we restructured Claude Code's CLAUDE.md files in a monorepo to cut 73% of always-loaded context without losing a single fact
- [Five Things I Learned About Making AI Coding Agents Actually Work](https://ivanmagda.dev/posts/five-things-ai-coding-agents-actually-work/): The biggest gains in AI agent performance come from the system around the model, not the model itself

## Optional

- [Source Code](https://github.com/ivan-magda/swift-claude-code): The swift-claude-code GitHub repository
